title: lamiaceae
intro: The Lamiaceae rank is a taxonomical family of flowering plants, commonly known as the mint family. This family is characterized by its distinctive flowers that are typically bilabiate, meaning they have two lips, and its aromatic leaves. The Lamiaceae family includes a diverse range of plants, such as mint, basil, rosemary, thyme, oregano, and lavender, among many others. With over 7,000 species, the Lamiaceae family is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants.
